items.name: Elixir
items.source_story_title: Elixir v1.20: Now a gradually typed language
items.summary: Elixir v1.20 — the language release with gradual typing.
items.why_included: Commenters praised the upgrade path and the compiler’s new bug-catching behavior.
items.evidence: A comment said updating Elixir had “no breaking changes across my many projects” and that the compiler “finds bugs for free.”

items.name: Gleam
items.source_story_title: Elixir v1.20: Now a gradually typed language
items.summary: Gleam — a typed BEAM language commenters held up as the Elixir alternative.
items.why_included: The thread framed it as the language to choose if you want types first.
items.evidence: One comment asked “why use Elixir over Gleam now?” and another said “if you don’t care about either of those things and only about types, use Gleam.”

items.name: Phoenix
items.source_story_title: Elixir v1.20: Now a gradually typed language
items.summary: Phoenix — the Elixir web framework with a strong reputation for macros and productivity.
items.why_included: Commenters pointed to Phoenix as a flagship Elixir library worth caring about.
items.evidence: A comment noted that libraries “such as Phoenix and Ecto” use macros “to great effect.”

items.name: DuckStation
items.summary: DuckStation — a PS1 emulator commenters called great on desktop.
items.why_included: It got an explicit positive recommendation alongside another respected emulator.
items.evidence: One comment said “PCSX-Redux [0] has been great on desktop, and DuckStation [1] as well.”

items.name: PCSX-Redux
items.summary: PCSX-Redux — a PlayStation emulator and reverse-engineering toolkit praised on HN.
items.why_included: Commenters singled it out as a solid desktop emulator/tooling option.
items.evidence: The thread described PCSX-Redux as “great on desktop” and linked it directly in the same praise as DuckStation.

items.name: Pink Trombone
items.source_story_title: What I've learned about the trombone
items.summary: Pink Trombone — an interactive vocal-tract simulator and music-education demo.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly linked it as a fun, concrete thing to try.
items.evidence: The thread included a direct “Pink Trombone https://dood.al/pinktrombone/” recommendation.

items.name: Kitten Space Agency
items.summary: Kitten Space Agency — a RocketWerkz space sim commenters told people to check out.
items.why_included: It was recommended directly to fans of space simulation games.
items.evidence: One comment said, “If any of you are fans of space simulator games, I’d check out Kitten Space Agency by RocketWerkz.”

items.name: SparkFX Studio
items.summary: SparkFX Studio — a Resolve workflow-automation tool pitched as a time saver.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as a practical editing accelerator.
items.evidence: A comment said it’s “an AI tool for helping speed up video workflows using agentic AI” and “presently in beta.”

items.name: Wireshark
items.source_story_title: Hacking your PC using your speaker without ever touching it
items.summary: Wireshark — the network analyzer one commenter used to reverse-engineer device traffic.
items.why_included: It was cited as a real tool used in the attack analysis.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote they used Wireshark to sniff the LED and EQ packets before writing a CLI utility in C.
